SEARCH
Package client.view

Class UserLoginController

java.lang.Object
client.view.UserLoginController
public class UserLoginController extends Object
Controller for UserLoginView.fxml — the combined connect + sign-in screen that runs at startup and again after logout. It collects the server host/port alongside the credentials: on submit it PING-probes the server (folding in what used to be a separate connection screen) and, once connected, sends LOGIN_STAFF or LOGIN_VISITOR via NetworkService. A Staff / Visitor segmented toggle swaps the visible credential field set.

The probe runs only when the session has no live connection yet (first login); a re-login after logout reuses the existing socket, so the server fields are pre-filled and locked then.

On success it stores the returned DTO in the Session and runs the supplied onSuccess callback (which opens the main shell). On failure it surfaces the server's message verbatim — including "Invalid username or password." and "This user is already logged in elsewhere."

For convenience while testing role-gated screens, a dev-only "Quick login" row of one-click buttons fills in and submits a seeded account (see quickAccounts()). It assumes the seed password changeme; the normal username/password fields are untouched and still work as before. The whole row is gated behind DEBUG_QUICK_LOGIN, which defaults to false so graders never see one-click role switching — flip it on locally while developing role-gated screens.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    private static final record 
    UserLoginController.Quick
    One seeded account reachable via a quick-login button: staff carry a username; visitors carry a visitorId (the other is null).

  • Field Details

    • DEBUG_QUICK_LOGIN

      private static final boolean DEBUG_QUICK_LOGIN
      Dev escape hatch: when true, the seeded-account "Quick login" row is built and shown. Keep this false for anything a grader will see.
      See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • UserLoginController

      public UserLoginController(NetworkService network, Session session, Runnable onSuccess)

      session - value supplied to the operation

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      private void initialize()
      Initializes the controller after its FXML fields are injected.

    • quickAccounts

      private List<UserLoginController.Quick> quickAccounts()
      The seeded accounts from setup.sql, one quick button each — every staff role and all three parks, plus a subscriber and a plain visitor. Staff carry a username (logged in with DEV_PASSWORD); the two visitor rows carry a national id. Managers/employees are labelled by their park so the park-scoped screens (approvals, occupancy, park params) are easy to reach.

    • buildQuickLoginButtons

      private void buildQuickLoginButtons()
      Performs the build quick login buttons operation.

    • quickLogin

      private void quickLogin(UserLoginController.Quick q)
      Fills in and submits a seeded account in one click. Staff accounts use the shared DEV_PASSWORD; visitor accounts log in by national id. The fields are populated (and the matching tab selected) so it stays visible which account was used and remains editable for a retry.

    • showStaff

      private void showStaff(boolean staff)
      Performs the show staff operation.
      Parameters:
      staff - value supplied to the operation

    • onSubmit

      private void onSubmit()
      Performs the on submit operation.

    • ensureConnectedThen

      private void ensureConnectedThen(Runnable submit)
      Connects first if there's no live socket yet (first login), then runs the credential submit; a re-login after logout reuses the existing connection and goes straight to submit.

    • isConnected

      private boolean isConnected()
      Returns:
      whether the session already holds a live server connection.

    • connectThen

      private void connectThen(Runnable after)
      PING-probes the entered host:port and, on success, promotes the connection into the Session and runs after (the credential submit). On failure it surfaces a reach error and re-enables the form. This is the old connection screen's probe, folded into this combined step.

    • submitStaff

      private void submitStaff()
      Performs the submit staff operation.

    • submitVisitor

      private void submitVisitor()
      Performs the submit visitor operation.
